title: Hydrophylax carnosa
intro: Hydrophylax carnosa, also known as the Red Milkberry, is a shrub-like plant species belonging to the family Diapensiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hydrophylax carnosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat skin conditions and other health-related issues.
